portfolio_rationale: LLY leads with the strongest fundamental growth story (36% revenue growth, consistent 3-week bullish signal history) and a bear case that fails to overcome the company's sector-specific demand drivers. AMZN and GOOGL both received bullish judge verdicts with strong multi-dimensional support and weak bear cases, warranting meaningful allocations. BRK.B and XOM are held at reduced weights due to extreme RSI readings (89.75 and 95.5 respectively) that signal near-term overbought risk despite bullish judge signals. AAPL is excluded entirely given its neutral judge signal and three consecutive neutral weeks.

LLY.reason: Strongest fundamental case with 36% revenue growth, consistent 3-week bullish signal history, and a bear case that relies only on elevated leverage and sector-agnostic macro headwinds.

AMZN.reason: Clean technical picture with MACD above signal, price above 20-day MA, positive news sentiment, and 10% revenue growth; bear case arguments are stale and contradicted by current data.

GOOGL.reason: Strong ROE, solid liquidity, all momentum indicators positive, and a 59% search interest spike support the bull case while the bear case rests only on RSI overbought and generic macro concerns.

BRK.B.reason: Bullish judge verdict with solid profitability metrics and positive news, but RSI at 89.75 is extremely overbought warranting a reduced position.

XOM.reason: Bullish judge verdict but RSI at 95.5 is the most extreme overbought reading in the universe and the fundamental case is thinner than peers, limiting conviction.

AAPL.reason: Judge signal is neutral with 0.5 confidence and three consecutive neutral weeks; excluded from portfolio.
